title: Race-lap ranking specialist with groupwise objective
summary: ROC AUC rewards ranking, and CatBoost exposes ranking losses plus native categorical handling; F1 strategy is also inherently relative to the other cars on the same lap rather than purely absolute.
rationale: ROC AUC rewards ranking, and CatBoost exposes ranking losses plus native categorical handling; F1 strategy is also inherently relative to the other cars on the same lap rather than purely absolute. A separate specialist that ranks drivers within `Race x Year x LapNumber` or `Race x Year x LapNumber x Compound regime` could capture who is most pit-imminent in that local decision set.
implementation_hint: Train a second model with group IDs built from race-lap contexts, using CatBoost ranking objectives such as `QueryCrossEntropy` or `YetiRank`, then blend its out-of-fold scores with the current binary classifier by rank-average or logistic stacking. Keep all categorical columns raw rather than manual one-hot.
expected_effect: Better ordering of borderline cases where several drivers are near the pit window at once, potentially improving public AUC even if logloss does not move.
risk: If groups are defined too narrowly or include leaked race aggregates, the model may overfit or learn unstable within-group quirks; ranking loss may also hurt absolute calibration, so blend rather than replace.
